problem_addressed: Language model agents lack specially-built interfaces for complex software engineering tasks, limiting their effectiveness compared to human engineers who benefit from sophisticated applications like IDEs.
core_claim: Custom agent-computer interfaces can significantly enhance language model agents' ability to perform software engineering tasks by providing LM-friendly commands and feedback formats.
key_contributions: Introduction of SWE-agent: a system facilitating LM agents to autonomously use computers for software engineering tasks
Design of custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enhances code creation, repository navigation, and test execution
Achieving state-of-the-art performance on SWE-bench (12.5% pass@1) and HumanEvalFix (87.7%) benchmarks
novelty: Unlike prior work where agents used existing applications like Linux shell or Python interpreter, SWE-agent introduces a purpose-built agent-computer interface specifically designed for LM agents performing software engineering tasks.
method_summary: SWE-agent implements a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that provides LM-friendly commands and environment feedback, enabling language model agents to interact with computers for software engineering tasks including file manipulation, repository navigation, and program execution.
method_components: Filesystem interaction capabilities for code creation and editing
Repository navigation and file management tools
Terminal interface for executing tests and programs
key_innovation: The agent-computer interface (ACI) that translates complex software engineering operations into LM-friendly commands and provides structured feedback, effectively bridging the gap between language model capabilities and software engineering requirements.
assumptions: Language model agents can effectively utilize computers when provided with appropriate interfaces
Interface design significantly impacts agent performance beyond just the underlying LM capabilities
LM agents represent a new category of end users with distinct needs from human users
theoretical_grounding: The paper does not provide explicit mathematical proofs or theoretical bounds, focusing instead on empirical evaluation of the proposed system's performance on established benchmarks.
headline_result: SWE-agent with GPT-4 resolved 286 trajectories on SWE-bench (full)
best_result: SWE-agent with GPT-4 resolved 286 trajectories on SWE-bench (full)
title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
